We therefore introduce a privilege-aware post-training defense that teaches agents to prefer sufficient lower-privilege tools and escalate only when necessary. Our mitigation experiments show that this defense substantially reduces unnecessary high-privilege tool use while preserving general capabilities.
affiliations: Institute for Artificial Intelligence, Peking University
Institute of Information Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences
School of Cyber Security, University of Chinese Academy of Sciences
The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Минимальные привилегии для LLM-агентов: защита от избыточного доступа
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема, когда LLM-агенты часто выбирают инструменты с избыточными привилегиями, даже когда доступны более простые альтернативы. Авторы создали ToolPrivBench — бенчмарк для оценки умения агентов выбирать инструменты с минимально необходимыми привилегиями, включая сценарии с временными отказами. Они обнаружили, что стандартное выравнивание безопасности (safety alignment) не гарантирует выбор инструментов с наименьшими привилегиями. В качестве решения предложена специализированная техника постобучения, которая обучает агентов предпочитать достаточные инструменты с низкими привилегиями и избегать эскалации без необходимости, сохраняя при этом общую производительность.

en.title: Empowering LLMs with Least-Privilege Tool Selection
en.desc: This paper addresses the issue of large language model (LLM) agents selecting tools with higher privileges than necessary, which can pose safety risks. The authors introduce ToolPrivBench, a framework to evaluate how often agents choose over-privileged tools when lower-privilege options are available. They find that this over-selection is common and worsens during transient tool failures. To combat this, they propose a post-training defense that encourages agents to prefer lower-privilege tools, effectively reducing unnecessary privilege use while maintaining performance.

zh.title: 优化工具选择，降低不必要的权限使用
zh.desc: 本论文研究了大型语言模型（LLM）代理在选择工具时常常不必要地选择高权限工具的问题。尽管安全对齐并不能确保选择最低权限的工具，但我们提出了一种后训练防御机制，可以减少过度权限的使用，同时不影响性能。我们开发了ToolPrivBench来评估代理在面对足够的低权限替代工具时，是否仍然选择高权限工具。实验结果表明，过度权限的选择在主流LLM代理中普遍存在，并且在工具暂时失效时更为严重。

papers.title: What Intermediate Layers Know: Detecting Jailbreaks from Entropy Dynamics
papers.abstract: Abstract Jailbreak attacks expose vulnerabilities in aligned large language models, revealing that harmful intent is encoded in structured intermediate uncertainty dynamics rather than output representations.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Jailbreak attacks reveal a persistent weakness in aligned Large Language Models: carefully crafted prompts can elicit policy-violating responses despite safety training. While most defenses operate at the prompt or output level, it remains unclear how harmful intent is encoded within the model's internal representations. We investigate this question by analyzing token-level predictive entropy trajectories across layers of a frozen LLM using the logit lens. We find that static aggregate statistics of prompt-level entropy (e.g., mean, variance) carry little discriminative signal, whereas features capturing how entropy evolves across token positions, such as monotonic rank-based trend scores, are substantially more informative. Importantly, this signal is not uniform across model depth: it is concentrated in intermediate layers and degrades at the final layer, indicating that jailbreak-relevant structure is most pronounced in mid-network representations rather than at the output head. Across multiple models (Llama, Qwen, Gemma) and adversarial benchmarks, these entropy dynamics provide architecture-consistent separation without additional training. Together, our findings show that jailbreak behavior is reflected in structured intermediate uncertainty dynamics, clarifying both which entropy-derived features encode harmful intent and where in the network that signal is most pronounced.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence
Skolkovo Institute of Science and Technology

ru.title: Вредоносный умысел скрывается в промежуточной неопределённости сети
ru.desc: В работе исследуется, как уязвимости к jailbreak-атакам кодируются во внутренних представлениях больших языковых моделей. Авторы анализируют динамику предсказательной энтропии токенов через слои модели, используя метод logit lens, и обнаруживают, что статические характеристики энтропии малоинформативны, а информативны признаки, отражающие эволюцию неопределённости на уровне позиций токенов. Ключевое открытие состоит в том, что сигнал о вредоносном намерении локализован в промежуточных слоях сети, а не в выходном слое, что указывает на специфическую структуру представлений, ответственных за jailbreak-поведение. Полученные закономерности в динамике энтропии демонстрируют состоятельность на различных моделях без дополнительного обучения и позволяют лучше понять механизм кодирования вредоносного поведения в LLM.

en.title: Unveiling Harmful Intent in Language Models Through Entropy Dynamics
en.desc: This paper investigates how jailbreak attacks exploit vulnerabilities in large language models (LLMs) by analyzing the internal representations of these models. It reveals that harmful intent is encoded in the model's intermediate layers, specifically through the dynamics of predictive entropy rather than just the final output. The study shows that traditional defenses focusing on prompts or outputs are insufficient, as the critical signals are found in how entropy evolves across token positions in the model. By examining multiple models, the authors demonstrate that understanding these intermediate uncertainty dynamics can help identify and mitigate jailbreak behaviors effectively.

zh.title: 揭示大型语言模型中的监控漏洞
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了大型语言模型中的监控漏洞，特别是如何通过精心设计的提示引发违反政策的响应。研究发现，模型内部的有害意图并不是通过输出表示编码的，而是通过中间层的不确定性动态来体现。通过分析模型各层的预测熵轨迹，发现中间层的熵动态特征比静态统计量更具信息量。我们的研究表明，监狱突破行为反映在结构化的中间不确定性动态中，揭示了有害意图的编码特征及其在网络中的位置。

papers.title: PrivacyAlign: Contextual Privacy Alignment for LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers develop a human-centered approach to align AI agents with privacy norms by creating a comprehensive dataset of privacy judgments and using annotation-conditioned reward modeling to improve agent behavior.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct AI agents acting on behalf of users are constantly making decisions, and for users to trust their agents, those decisions must align with what they actually want. Privacy is an important alignment problem for agents: every message, post, or tool call an agent makes is a contextual judgment about what is appropriate to share, with whom, and under which conditions. Because such judgments depend on social expectations and norms, human judgment does not merely label privacy violations but also helps define them. While existing work relies on unreliable proxies for both training and evaluation, we place human judgment at the center of agentic privacy alignment. We introduce PrivacyAlign, a dataset of 1,350 samples with 3,516 detailed annotations from 599 unique annotators across diverse scenarios where current LLMs actually leak, and use it to ground both alignment training and automated evaluation in human privacy norms. Building on these annotations, we first show that conditioning LLM judges on human annotations and explanations for reference responses to the same prompt makes their judgments more reliable. We then introduce annotation-conditioned reward modeling, which uses these annotations to score new responses during RL, and show that small open-weight agents trained with this reward better align with human privacy norms, with strong gains on PrivacyAlign and existing privacy benchmarks for agents.

ru.title: Выравнивание AI агентов с человеческими нормами конфиденциальности через аннотированное обучение с подкреплением
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали подход, ориентированный на человека, для выравнивания поведения AI агентов с нормами конфиденциальности. Они создали датасет PrivacyAlign с 1350 образцами и 3516 аннотаций от 599 аннотаторов, охватывающий реальные сценарии утечек данных. На основе этих аннотаций авторы предложили метод conditioning reward modeling, который использует человеческие суждения для оценки ответов во время reinforcement learning. Эксперименты показали, что небольшие open-weight модели, обученные с помощью такого подхода, значительно лучше выравниваются с человеческими нормами конфиденциальности по сравнению с существующими методами.

en.title: Aligning AI with Human Privacy Norms
en.desc: This paper presents a human-centered method to ensure that AI agents respect privacy norms by creating a dataset called PrivacyAlign, which includes privacy judgments from a diverse group of annotators. The dataset consists of 1,350 samples and 3,516 annotations that help define what constitutes a privacy violation based on social expectations. The authors introduce annotation-conditioned reward modeling, which improves the training of AI agents by using human judgments to guide their decision-making processes. The results show that AI agents trained with this approach better align with human privacy expectations, demonstrating significant improvements in both the new dataset and existing benchmarks.

zh.title: 以人为本的隐私对齐方法
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种以人为中心的方法，以使人工智能代理与隐私规范保持一致。研究团队创建了一个包含1350个样本和3516个详细注释的隐私判断数据集，旨在帮助代理做出更符合用户期望的决策。通过将人类判断置于隐私对齐的中心，研究展示了如何利用注释条件的奖励建模来提高代理的行为表现。最终，经过训练的小型开放权重代理在隐私对齐和现有隐私基准测试中表现出显著的改进。

papers.title: Lite Any Stereo V2: Faster and Stronger Efficient Zero-Shot Stereo Matching
papers.abstract: Abstract Lite Any Stereo V2 (LAS2) presents an efficient stereo matching approach that achieves state-of-the-art accuracy with significantly reduced latency through optimized architecture and training strategies.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Recent advances in stereo matching have achieved remarkable accuracy, but often rely on large models, heavy computation, or additional foundation-model priors, making them difficult to deploy on resource-constrained platforms. In contrast, efficient stereo models offer faster inference but are commonly considered less capable of strong zero-shot generalization. In this paper, we challenge this assumption by introducing Lite Any Stereo V2 (LAS2), an ultra-fast model series designed for efficient zero-shot stereo matching. LAS2 is developed from both architecture and training perspectives. Architecturally, we revisit efficient stereo design under practical deployment settings and propose a 2D-only cost aggregation framework, optimized for real inference latency rather than theoretical MACs alone. For training, we develop a three-stage strategy that combines synthetic supervision, self-distillation, and real-world knowledge distillation. To improve the reliability of real-world pseudo supervision, we further introduce pseudo-label filtering and an error-clamping operation, enabling smoother synthetic-to-real transfer. We instantiate LAS2 as a family of models, including feed-forward variants for different efficiency budgets and an iterative variant for higher accuracy. Extensive experiments show that LAS2 achieves state-of-the-art accuracy among efficient stereo methods while maintaining significantly lower latency. Specifically, LAS2-H achieves stronger overall zero-shot performance than the iterative method Fast-FoundationStereo, with 1.8x and 2.7x faster inference on H200 and Orin, respectively. The project page, demos, and code are available at https://tomtomtommi.github.io/LiteAnyStereoV2/.

ru.title: Быстрая стереовизия без компромиссов в качестве
ru.desc: В статье представлена Lite Any Stereo V2 (LAS2) — серия эффективных моделей для стереоматчинга, которые достигают высокой точности при низкой латентности благодаря оптимизированной архитектуре и специальным стратегиям обучения. Архитектурно модель использует двумерную агрегацию стоимостей, оптимизированную для реальной скорости вычисления, а не только теоретических операций. Обучение проводится в три этапа: синтетическая супервизия, самодистилляция и дистилляция знаний на реальных данных с фильтрацией псевдо-меток. LAS2 демонстрирует лучшую обобщаемость в нулевой выстрел, сохраняя при этом 1.8-2.7x ускорение по сравнению с существующими методами на различных платформах.

en.title: Efficient Stereo Matching Redefined with LAS2
en.desc: Lite Any Stereo V2 (LAS2) introduces a new method for stereo matching that balances high accuracy with low latency, making it suitable for devices with limited resources. The model is designed using a 2D-only cost aggregation framework that prioritizes real-world inference speed over theoretical performance metrics. It employs a unique three-stage training strategy that includes synthetic supervision and self-distillation to enhance its zero-shot generalization capabilities. LAS2 demonstrates superior performance compared to existing efficient models, achieving faster inference times while maintaining state-of-the-art accuracy.

zh.title: 高效立体匹配的新突破
zh.desc: Lite Any Stereo V2 (LAS2) 是一种高效的立体匹配方法，能够在显著降低延迟的同时实现最先进的准确性。该模型通过优化架构和训练策略，克服了传统大模型在资源受限平台上的部署难题。LAS2 采用了二维成本聚合框架，并结合合成监督、自我蒸馏和真实世界知识蒸馏的三阶段训练策略，提升了模型的零-shot 泛化能力。实验结果表明，LAS2 在高效立体方法中实现了最佳准确性，同时保持了显著更低的推理延迟。

papers.title: Plans Don't Persist: Why Context Management Is Load Bearing for LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract Standard LLM agents rely on plan content remaining in context rather than maintaining it as persistent state, with evidence shown through replay pairing diagnostics and compression stress tests.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Long-horizon agents depend on context management: systems compress, summarize, and evict old tokens so tasks can continue beyond finite windows. That is safe only when dropped information is no longer needed or has been internalized. Plans are the stress case: they are written early, used for many steps, and first to be evicted. We introduce replay pairing, a diagnostic that runs the same trajectory with and without the plan in history and measures hidden-state cosine distance. On Llama-3.1-70B, plan signal spikes to 0.453 one step after the plan, then falls 4.1x in a single action-observation step; HotpotQA falls 12.4x. This is evidence that standard LLM agents do not carry plans forward as persistent state, and instead depend on the plan remaining in context. A layer-L32 probe detects this decay as a diagnostic, not as proof that it reads plan content itself. Reasoning models add a measurement confound: their `<think>` traces re-derive plan content, so standard stripping leaves plan evidence in the stripped condition. We name this the reasoning-trace confound and fix it with strict stripping, which removes prior `<think>` blocks from the stripped run only. It recovers +163% of the step+1 signal in-sample and +153% held out, while not meaningfully changing non-reasoning Llama (+4.8%). On DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Llama-70B, a Llama-trained probe transfers at AUROC 0.748 (p=6e-4), while R1-specific probes reach 1.000, suggesting R1 encodes plan signal in a different hidden-state direction. Finally, a compression stress test shows the practical cost: naive plan eviction cuts ALFWorld success by 34.7pp, while probe-gated re-surfacing does not recover it. The contribution is a measurement and stress-test framework showing that agent-critical information can be context-resident rather than persistent. Context management is load bearing, but plan protection alone is not enough.

ru.title: Планы в контексте: почему LLM агенты забывают свои стратегии
ru.desc: В статье исследуется, как языковые модели большого размера (LLM) обрабатывают планы при выполнении задач на длительных горизонтах. Авторы вводят метод replay pairing для диагностики, который сравнивает скрытые состояния агента с планом и без него в контексте. Результаты показывают, что стандартные LLM агенты не сохраняют планы как персистентное состояние, а полагаются на наличие плана в контекстном окне. Исследование также демонстрирует практические последствия: удаление плана из контекста снижает успешность решения задач на 34.7 процентных пункта, что подчеркивает критическую важность управления контекстом для агентов.

en.title: Context is Key: Protecting Plans in LLMs
en.desc: This paper discusses how standard large language model (LLM) agents manage plans during task execution. It reveals that these agents rely on keeping plans in context rather than maintaining them as a persistent state, which can lead to significant information loss. The authors introduce a diagnostic method called replay pairing to measure how well plans are retained in the model's hidden states. They also highlight the importance of context management and propose that simply evicting plans can severely impact the performance of agents in complex tasks.

zh.title: 上下文管理：计划保护的关键
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了标准大型语言模型（LLM）代理如何依赖上下文管理而非持久状态来处理计划内容。研究表明，计划信息在使用过程中会被压缩和清除，导致其在关键时刻的丢失。通过引入重放配对诊断，作者测量了计划信号的衰减，发现标准LLM代理并不将计划作为持久状态，而是依赖于上下文中的计划信息。最后，论文提出了一种测量和压力测试框架，强调了上下文管理在代理性能中的重要性。

papers.title: Distill Once, Adapt Life-Long: Exploring Dataset Distillation for Continual Test-Time Adaptation
papers.abstract: Abstract DO-ALL is a test-time adaptation framework that uses dataset distillation to create synthetic anchors for stable long-term model performance without retaining source data.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Continual Test-Time Adaptation (CTTA) aims to maintain model performance under evolving target domains by adapting online without labeled data. However, practical deployments often cannot retain the source dataset due to privacy or licensing constraints, and purely source-free CTTA methods tend to become unstable under long-term distribution shift, suffering from compounding self-training errors and catastrophic forgetting. We introduce DO-ALL (Distill Once, Adapt Life-Long), a plug-and-play framework that revisits source information in a compact and privacy-conscious form via Dataset Distillation (DD). Before deployment, DO-ALL performs DD to produce a small set of synthetic distilled anchors that summarize the source distribution. During adaptation, each target sample is matched with its most semantically aligned anchor, which provides a stable reference for various CTTA via source replay, representation alignment, and manifold-smoothing regularization. DO-ALL can be seamlessly integrated into existing CTTA algorithms, consistently improving long-term robustness across CIFAR100-C, ImageNet-C, and the CCC benchmark. This demonstrates the potential of leveraging DD to enable stable and continuous adaptation without retaining raw source data. The code is available at https://github.com/blue-531/DOALL.

ru.title: Дистиллированные якоря для вечной адаптации модели без исходных данных
ru.desc: DO-ALL представляет фреймворк для адаптации моделей во время тестирования, который использует дистилляцию датасета для создания синтетических якорей без сохранения исходных данных. Метод решает проблему нестабильности при долгосрочной адаптации к смещению распределения целевых данных, предотвращая накопление ошибок самообучения и катастрофическое забывание. Синтетические якоря используются как стабильные ориентиры при помощи механизмов источника-повтора, выравнивания представлений и регуляризации многообразия. Подход интегрируется в существующие алгоритмы непрерывной адаптации и демонстрирует улучшение робастности на бенчмарках CIFAR100-C, ImageNet-C и CCC без необходимости хранить исходные данные.

en.title: Stable Adaptation Without Source Data
en.desc: DO-ALL is a framework designed for continual test-time adaptation (CTTA) that enhances model performance over time without needing to keep the original training data. It uses dataset distillation to create a small set of synthetic anchors that represent the source data, allowing the model to adapt to new, evolving target domains. This approach helps prevent issues like catastrophic forgetting and self-training errors that can occur when adapting without source data. By integrating DO-ALL into existing CTTA methods, it improves long-term stability and robustness across various benchmarks.

zh.title: DO-ALL：无源数据的长期适应新方法
zh.desc: DO-ALL是一个测试时适应框架，利用数据集蒸馏技术生成合成锚点，以实现长期模型性能的稳定，而无需保留源数据。该框架旨在在不断变化的目标领域中，通过在线适应来维持模型性能，且不依赖标记数据。DO-ALL通过数据集蒸馏在紧凑且注重隐私的形式中重新访问源信息，生成一小组合成蒸馏锚点。它可以无缝集成到现有的持续测试时适应算法中，显著提高长期鲁棒性。

papers.title: Constraint Tax in Open-Weight LLMs: An Empirical Study of Tool Calling Suppression Under Structured Output Constraints
papers.abstract: Abstract Tool Suppression occurs when JSON Schema constraints and tool calling are jointly enabled, preventing open-weight models from invoking tools despite maintaining schema compliance, with the issue stemming from grammar-based token masking that makes tool-call tokens unreachable during decoding.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Tool Calling and Structured Output are two core capabilities of modern Agent systems, yet their interaction under joint deployment conditions remains insufficiently understood. This paper reports a reproducible phenomenon observed in a production Agent system: when Tool Calling and JSON Schema constraints are simultaneously enabled, multiple open-weight models cease invoking tools despite maintaining high schema compliance. We refer to this behavior as Tool Suppression. Through controlled experiments across multiple model families and deployment settings, we consistently reproduce Tool Suppression under joint constraints, while tool execution and schema compliance remain functional when evaluated independently. Further analysis reveals that JSON Schema constraints are compiled into grammar-based token masks, causing tool-call tokens to become unreachable during decoding. This provides an implementation-level explanation for the observed behavior. To interpret the phenomenon, we formulate the Constraint Priority Inversion (CPI) hypothesis, which suggests that schema satisfaction may dominate action-selection behavior under multiple simultaneous constraints. We present CPI as a behavioral hypothesis consistent with the observed evidence rather than a verified internal mechanism. To mitigate the problem, we propose Transparent Two-Pass Execution, an inference-time strategy that decouples tool execution from schema-constrained response generation. Experimental results show that this approach restores tool invocation while preserving structured output guarantees without requiring model retraining. These findings suggest that evaluating tool use and structured output separately may overlook important reliability issues in production Agent systems. Code, data, and docs will be released at https://github.com/Fzsama/Constrain-Tax-26-06.git.
affiliations: Focus AI Center, Focus Technology Co., Ltd.
Nanjing University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Когда ограничения схемы блокируют вызовы функций в языковых моделях
ru.desc: В работе описывается явление Tool Suppression, когда одновременное использование JSON Schema ограничений и инструментов вызовов приводит к тому, что открытые языковые модели перестают вызывать функции, несмотря на соблюдение схемы. Авторы показали, что проблема возникает из-за грамматического маскирования токенов, которое делает токены вызова инструментов недостижимыми во время декодирования. Они предложили гипотезу инверсии приоритета ограничений, объясняющую, почему удовлетворение схеме доминирует над выбором действий при множественных одновременных ограничениях. Для решения проблемы предложена стратегия двухпроходного исполнения, которая отделяет выполнение инструментов от генерации структурированного выхода без переобучения модели.

en.title: Unblocking Tool Invocation in ML Models
en.desc: This paper discusses a problem called Tool Suppression, which occurs when both JSON Schema constraints and tool calling are enabled in machine learning models. Despite the models being compliant with the schema, they fail to invoke tools due to grammar-based token masking that makes tool-call tokens inaccessible during the decoding process. The authors introduce the Constraint Priority Inversion (CPI) hypothesis to explain this behavior, suggesting that satisfying schema constraints can interfere with the model's ability to select actions. To address this issue, they propose a solution called Transparent Two-Pass Execution, which allows for effective tool invocation while maintaining structured output without needing to retrain the models.

zh.title: 工具调用与Schema约束的相互影响
zh.desc: 本文探讨了工具抑制现象，即在同时启用JSON Schema约束和工具调用时，开放权重模型无法调用工具，尽管仍然符合Schema要求。研究发现，这种现象源于基于语法的令牌掩码，使得工具调用令牌在解码过程中变得不可达。为了解释这一现象，提出了约束优先级反转（CPI）假设，认为在多个约束同时存在时，Schema的满足可能主导行动选择行为。为了解决这一问题，提出了透明的双通道执行策略，能够在不重新训练模型的情况下恢复工具调用，同时保持结构化输出的保证。

papers.title: Speaker Identity in Non-Verbal Vocalizations: Conditional Distillation and Mixture of Experts Approach
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel speaker verification framework combines frozen self-supervised features with ECAPA-TDNN and MoE modules to improve identity verification across both speech and non-verbal vocalizations while maintaining speech performance.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct As expressive text-to-speech (TTS) and voice conversion (VC) systems increasingly generate non-verbal vocalizations (NVVs) to enhance naturalness, reliable speaker verification (SV) becomes essential to objectively assess identity consistency across both verbal and non-verbal segments. Yet current SV systems generalize poorly to NVVs, and fine-tuning on NVV data causes catastrophic forgetting of speech performance. We present the first systematic study across 10 NVV types and propose a framework combining frozen Data2Vec self-supervised features with ECAPA-TDNN, enhanced by a Mixture of Experts (MoE) module with learned domain-aware routing. A conditional distillation loss on speech inputs via a pretrained teacher retains speech-to-speech accuracy, while a contrastive loss bridges the speech-NVV domain gap. Our method reduces speech-NVV EER from 38.93% to 22.66% over a pretrained baseline, and improves speech EER from 13.17% to 9.24% via distillation.
affiliations: National Taiwan University Artificial Intelligence Center of Research Excellence, Taipei, Taiwan
Signal Analysis and Interpretation Laboratory (SAIL), University of Southern California, USA

ru.title: Универсальная верификация говорящего для речи и невербальных вокализаций
ru.desc: Работа представляет новую систему верификации говорящего, которая эффективно работает как с речью, так и с невербальными вокализациями (смех, вздохи и т.д.). Основной подход использует замороженные признаки из самообучающейся модели Data2Vec в сочетании с архитектурой ECAPA-TDNN и модулем Mixture of Experts для маршрутизации между областями. Для предотвращения катастрофического забывания речевых навыков применяется дистилляция знаний от предобученной модели и контрастивная потеря для сближения представлений речи и невербальных звуков. Метод значительно снижает ошибку верификации при переходе между речью и невербальными вокализациями с 38.93% до 22.66%, одновременно улучшая качество на чистой речи.

en.title: Bridging Speech and Non-Verbal Vocalizations for Better Speaker Verification
en.desc: This paper introduces a new framework for speaker verification that effectively handles both speech and non-verbal vocalizations (NVVs). It utilizes frozen self-supervised features from Data2Vec and integrates them with ECAPA-TDNN and a Mixture of Experts (MoE) module to enhance identity verification. The framework addresses the challenge of generalization to NVVs without sacrificing performance on speech data, using a conditional distillation loss to maintain accuracy. The results show significant improvements in error rates for both speech and NVV verification tasks, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed approach.

zh.title: 提升说话人验证的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的说话人验证框架，结合了冻结的自监督特征与ECAPA-TDNN和混合专家（MoE）模块，以提高语音和非语言发声的身份验证效果，同时保持语音性能。随着文本到语音（TTS）和语音转换（VC）系统生成非语言发声（NVVs）以增强自然性，可靠的说话人验证变得至关重要。现有的说话人验证系统在NVVs上泛化能力较差，且在NVV数据上微调会导致语音性能的灾难性遗忘。我们的方法通过预训练的教师模型进行条件蒸馏损失，保持了语音到语音的准确性，同时通过对比损失缩小了语音与NVV之间的领域差距。

papers.title: Forecasting Future Behavior as a Learning Task
papers.abstract: Abstract Behavior Forecasters are trained to predict large reasoning model outputs from single trajectories, outperforming large language models while requiring significantly less computational cost.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Trust in an AI system is often anchored by explanations of how it works, which one then uses to forecast its behavior on new inputs. For large reasoning models (LRMs), this conventional route is particularly difficult to follow: explanation methods for single token generations do not naturally generalize to long trajectories, and the trajectories themselves are often not faithful when read as natural language. We propose an alternative that bypasses the explanation step: treat behavior forecasting as a learnable task and train Behavior Forecasters that operates on a single reasoning trajectory to make the same forecasts one would typically seek from an explanation. The forecaster's training data is obtained by querying the LRM with no human annotation, and its inference is done in a single forward pass. We instantiate this approach on two tasks: how likely the LRM is to repeat its answer on re-runs, and how removing parts of the input changes its answer. We evaluate this approach on both tasks across three diverse reasoning datasets and find that trained Behavior Forecasters are more accurate than GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus-4.6 reading the same trajectories as naive readers, at a small fraction of their inference cost. We find that fine-tuning the backbone end-to-end and initializing it from the target LRM are each necessary for strong performance. These results show that the reasoning trajectory carries information about the LRM's future behavior that goes beyond what naive reading conveys.

ru.title: Предсказание поведения моделей через обучение на траекториях рассуждений
ru.desc: Статья предлагает новый подход к прогнозированию поведения больших моделей рассуждения (LRM) путём обучения специальных Behavior Forecaster моделей на основе единственной траектории рассуждений, без необходимости объяснять работу модели в понятных человеку терминах. Авторы показали, что обученные предсказатели поведения превосходят по точности напрямую промпты к GPT-5.4 и Claude Opus при значительно меньших вычислительных затратах, так как требуют только один forward pass. Модели обучаются на данных, полученных запросами к целевой LRM без ручной аннотации, и выполняют два задания: предсказание повторяемости ответов модели и оценку влияния удаления частей входных данных на результаты. Результаты демонстрируют, что траектория рассуждений содержит информацию о будущем поведении LRM, которая не доступна при наивном чтении текста.

en.title: Predicting AI Behavior with Less Cost and More Accuracy
en.desc: This paper introduces Behavior Forecasters, a new method for predicting the outputs of large reasoning models (LRMs) using single trajectories. Unlike traditional explanation methods that struggle with long sequences, Behavior Forecasters learn to forecast behavior directly from the trajectory data without requiring human annotations. The approach is evaluated on tasks such as predicting answer repetition and the impact of input changes, demonstrating superior accuracy compared to existing models like GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus-4.6. The findings suggest that the reasoning trajectory contains valuable information about the LRM's future behavior that is not captured by naive reading methods.

zh.title: 行为预测：超越传统解释的智能预测
zh.desc: 行为预测器被训练用来从单一轨迹预测大型推理模型的输出，表现优于大型语言模型，同时计算成本显著降低。传统的解释方法在处理长轨迹时难以适用，因此我们提出了一种新方法，将行为预测视为一个可学习的任务。通过对大型推理模型进行查询，生成训练数据，而无需人工标注，预测过程只需一次前向传播。我们的实验表明，训练后的行为预测器在准确性上超过了其他大型语言模型，并且在推理成本上大幅降低。
